rationale: Y is stronger overall because it states an explicit objective and identifies the primary endpoint, which makes the study aim and endpoint hierarchy clearer. Both abstracts have a specific PFA redo-ablation gap and report the main 12-month freedom result with CI and p value, but both weaken the RF comparison by saying procedure and dwelling times were significantly shorter without giving effect sizes, matched sample size, or p values. Certainty is moderate in both: feasibility and 1-year effectiveness are supported, while safety claims are somewhat strong for 117 patients and one major complication.
loser_weakness: X most lacked explicit endpoint framing and a separate objective statement, making the comparison and primary outcome less transparent than Y.
